title: Shipyard user-global config
description: Schema for ~/.shipyard/config.json (user-global, applies to every repo unless overridden by the repo-level shipyard.config.json or the per-repo .shipyard/config.local.json).

$schema.description: Optional schema reference for editor autocomplete.

authors.description: User-global trusted-author list. Accepts any GitHub login shape — plain logins (mattsears18), REST bot logins (sentry[bot]), and GraphQL app logins (app/sentry) — since the field is only a comparison key: an entry that matches nothing is a harmless no-op, so no pattern constraint is enforced (see issue #371). Repo-level trust.authors overrides this (does not merge).

default_auto_merge_policy.description: Default auto-merge policy across every repo. Remapped onto the canonical auto_merge.policy path on load (shipyard-config.sh normalize_user_layer, issue #403), so it overrides the built-in default; repo-level / local auto_merge.policy still wins.

default_models.description: Default per-mode model IDs across every repo. Remapped onto the canonical models.* paths on load (shipyard-config.sh normalize_user_layer, issue #403); an explicit repo/local models.<mode> still wins. Honored at dispatch time by resolve-dispatch-model.sh, which maps the id onto the Agent tool's model parameter (issue #727) — one key per worker mode, matching models.* in the repo schema.

cost_tracking_enabled.description: Default-on cost-tracking switch across every repo. Remapped onto the canonical cost_tracking.enabled path on load (shipyard-config.sh normalize_user_layer, issue #403); repo-level / local cost_tracking.enabled overrides this.
